campaigns.name: PA Outreach — CFO / VP Finance / FP&A

emails.subject: quick question about your close cycle
emails.body: [First Name], Most finance leaders I talk to in Florida are spending 3-4 weeks on quarterly forecasting. The data exists — it's just scattered across systems, and pulling it together manually is where the time goes. We helped Idaho Forest Group get from 80+ hours of spreadsheet work down to under 15 per cycle. Same data, just connected. Worth a 15-minute call to see if that'd translate to your setup? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: the forecast accuracy piece
emails.body: [First Name], Sent you a note last week — one more thing worth flagging. The teams we work with usually find that forecast accuracy gets worse during peak planning, not better. More pressure, more manual touchpoints, more room for error. Idaho Forest hit 97% accuracy after moving off spreadsheets. Their team stopped fixing numbers and started actually analyzing them. Are you the right person to have this conversation, or should I reach out to someone else on your team? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: Idaho Forest: 80 hours to 15
emails.body: [First Name], Idaho Forest Group was spending 80+ hours a month on forecasting — manual pulls, spreadsheet reconciliation, coordinating across departments. Reactive mode every quarter. After IBM Planning Analytics: under 15 hours. 97% accuracy. Quarters closed faster. Happy to walk you through what changed. 20 minutes next week? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: closing the loop
emails.body: [First Name], Tried you a couple times — no worries if the timing's off. If forecasting cycles aren't a priority right now, totally understand. If they are and I just caught you at a bad time, I'm easy to reach. Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

campaigns.name: PA Outreach — VP of IT / CTO

emails.subject: finance's spreadsheet problem landing on IT
emails.body: [First Name], Most IT leaders I talk to are dealing with the same thing — finance is running planning off a mix of ERP exports, spreadsheets, and whatever FP&A built five years ago. IT ends up owning the mess. IBM Planning Analytics connects directly into your existing stack — SAP, Oracle, Workday — and gives finance a governed single source of truth. No more shadow systems. Can we grab 15 minutes to talk through what that looks like technically? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: re: the data governance piece
emails.body: [First Name], Following up from last week. The IT leaders I talk to care about two things: not adding another siloed tool, and not owning finance's data problems forever. Planning Analytics sits in your governed environment — finance gets self-service, your team controls the data layer. Not another rogue SaaS subscription. Worth a quick call? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: how ICBC handled it
emails.body: [First Name], ICBC runs Planning Analytics across their planning function — consolidated data, one governed platform, finance self-sufficient without IT in the loop for every report. They hit 97% forecast accuracy. More importantly, IT stopped getting pulled into spreadsheet firefighting every quarter. Happy to walk through the architecture. 20 minutes? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: last note
emails.body: [First Name], Last one from me. If the timing or priority isn't there, no problem. If you're dealing with planning data sprawl and want to see how others solved it, I'm around. Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

campaigns.name: PA Outreach — CRO / Chief Revenue Officer

emails.subject: pipeline forecasting that actually holds
emails.body: [First Name], Most revenue leaders I talk to have the same issue — forecast looks solid in the CRM but doesn't match what closes. The gap usually comes down to how pipeline data rolls up across teams. IBM Planning Analytics connects revenue data and lets you model scenarios in real time. Not just reporting — actually planning. We helped a financial services firm hit 97% forecast accuracy. Open to a quick call? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: the quota-to-close gap
emails.body: [First Name], Sent you a note last week. One thing worth adding — Most CROs find their biggest forecasting problem isn't data quality, it's the time it takes to reconcile and model. By the time a clean number surfaces, the window's gone. Planning Analytics cuts that cycle significantly. Your team spends less time building the forecast and more time acting on it. Does this hit close to home, or is your planning process already dialed in? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: 97% forecast accuracy — how they got there
emails.body: [First Name], ICBC was dealing with the same forecasting whiplash most revenue orgs face — numbers shifting late in the quarter, limited ability to model scenarios fast. After IBM Planning Analytics: 97% accuracy, planning cycles 25% shorter. Happy to walk through what that looked like. 20 minutes next week? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: wrapping up
emails.body: [First Name], Last note — reached out a few times about tightening up revenue forecasting. If it's not the right time or someone else owns this, happy to redirect. Otherwise, easy to reach whenever the timing works. Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

campaigns.name: PA Outreach — Director of Marketing / CMO

emails.subject: marketing budget planning that doesn't take all quarter
emails.body: [First Name], Marketing planning is one of the messiest processes to get right — budget allocation, campaign ROI modeling, coordinating with finance on approvals. Most teams are still doing it in spreadsheets. IBM Planning Analytics connects spend data, attribution models, and budget cycles in one place. Finance and marketing off the same numbers. Worth a quick conversation? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: the spend attribution piece
emails.body: [First Name], Sent you a note last week. The marketing leaders I talk to usually have two problems: they can't see ROI fast enough to reallocate budget mid-quarter, and getting finance to approve changes takes too long because the data isn't in a format they trust. Planning Analytics solves both — real-time visibility, shared model with finance. Is this something your team is actively working on, or still in the 'dealing with it' phase? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: cut planning time by 25% — how one team did it
emails.body: [First Name], One of the teams we work with cut their planning cycle by 25% after moving budget and campaign planning into IBM Planning Analytics. Less time in spreadsheets, more time on strategy. Finance trusted the numbers because they came from a shared system. Happy to walk you through it. 20 minutes this week? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: last one
emails.body: [First Name], Last note on this. If marketing planning and budget coordination are running smoothly, great — no need to fix what works. If there's still friction with finance or visibility gaps mid-quarter, I'm easy to reach. Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

campaigns.name: PA Outreach — CHRO / VP of HR

emails.subject: headcount planning eating your team's time?
emails.body: [First Name], Workforce planning is one of the harder processes to get right — headcount models, attrition scenarios, budget alignment with finance, all on different spreadsheets. IBM Planning Analytics brings it together. HR and finance on a single model, scenarios updated in real time instead of rebuilt from scratch every quarter. We've helped companies run planning cycles 25% faster. Worth a quick call? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: the headcount-to-budget alignment problem
emails.body: [First Name], Reached out last week. The HR leaders I talk to usually hit the same wall — they build a headcount plan, finance adjusts it, HR rebuilds, back and forth. Takes weeks and the final number satisfies nobody. Planning Analytics gives HR and finance a shared model so those cycles collapse. You stop negotiating over spreadsheets. Is headcount planning a pain point for your team right now? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: faster workforce planning — how others got there
emails.body: [First Name], Teams on IBM Planning Analytics are seeing 25% faster planning cycles and a lot less back-and-forth with finance. The difference is a shared data model — HR inputs headcount, finance sees it in real time, scenarios update automatically. No version control nightmares. Happy to walk through what that looks like. 20 minutes? Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

emails.subject: closing out
emails.body: [First Name], Last note from me. If workforce planning is running well, I'll leave you alone. If headcount cycles, budget alignment, or scenario planning are still painful, happy to reconnect whenever. Bradley Milks IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning bradley.milks@ibm.com

persona_emails.subject: Finance planning — quick question
persona_emails.body:
Hi [First Name],

Most CFOs I speak with are dealing with the same challenge — disconnected planning and forecast cycles that can't keep up with how fast conditions change.

IBM Planning Analytics unifies financial planning on one real-time platform, integrating Oracle, SAP, and spreadsheets so your FP&A team moves faster with full confidence in the numbers.

Worth 20 minutes for [Company]?

Bradley Milks
Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning | IBM | Florida
